findings.title: Double type assertion `as unknown as Note` masks field shape mismatch
findings.why_it_matters: Casting `local as unknown as Note` and `record as unknown as Note` silently bypasses TypeScript's ability to catch field mismatches between the Dexie row shape (Record<string,unknown>) and the Note interface. If a downloaded record from Supabase is missing required Note fields (e.g., no `tags` array after toCamelCase), applyConflictCopy will create a conflictCopy.tags that is undefined, which will propagate to IDB and potentially crash the dialog render.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a narrow type guard or zod/assertion check before casting: verify `record['tags']` is an array and `record['content']` is a string before invoking applyConflictCopy. Alternatively, make _applyConflictCopy work with Record<string,unknown> directly without casting to Note.
evidence: syncEngine.ts line 533: `applyConflictCopy(local as unknown as Note, record as unknown as Note)` — double assertion bypasses all type checking
conflictResolvers.ts line 29: `tags: local.tags` — if local.tags is undefined (missing from IDB record), conflictCopy.tags will be undefined, not []
NoteConflictDialog.tsx line 264: `conflictCopy?.content.replace(...)` — if conflictCopy.content is undefined, this throws TypeError

findings.title: Note.conflictCopy undefined/null semantic is correct but unusual
findings.why_it_matters: The three-state semantic (undefined=never conflicted, null=resolved, object=active) is well documented in the type comment. However, all UI checks use `!= null` which also catches undefined. If any code path attempts to serialize `conflictCopy: undefined` to JSON (e.g., for localStorage or postMessage), the field is silently dropped. This could cause a conflict to reappear after serialization round-trip.
findings.suggested_fix: The current usage is safe for IDB and Supabase (which handle undefined correctly). If the Note type is ever used in JSON serialization contexts, normalize conflictCopy: undefined → null before serialization.
evidence: types.ts line 465: `conflictCopy?: { content: string; tags: string[]; savedAt: string } | null` — optional means undefined is allowed
NoteCard.tsx line 88: `note.conflictCopy != null` — correctly handles both undefined and null (badge hidden in both cases)
No serialization path currently converts Note to JSON string, so risk is theoretical

residual_risks: conflictResolvers.ts is correctly a pure module (no Dexie/Supabase/React imports). The `import type { Note }` is type-only and stripped at compile time. Purity constraint is met.
The explicit fieldMap entry for conflictCopy is redundant with auto camelCase but harmless — TypeScript type safety is unaffected.
testing_gaps: No test for applyConflictCopy receiving a local record with undefined tags (missing field from older IDB schema)
